titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L141
GA L141
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Supplied text at 19.11 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 W1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L141Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rΒase text: TRnsΤranscribed by: acmΤranscription begun: 5.7.12Τranscription finished: 9.11.12Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - NoAre there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses:5.4 No7.53 - 8.11 NoOther remarks:10 Unidentified readings in this transcription.
1. συναξαριον i: πασχα to πεντηκοστη - ευαγγελια κατα ιωαννην - S1 W1 D1 - S1 W8 D1 Easter to Pentecost

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
John 6,48-54 (According to Scrivener, Gale transposes with W3 D4)
John 5,1-15 Fourth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman
John 14,1-11a (Scrivener John 14,1-10; Gale John 14,1-11, Wake John 14,1-12)
John 14,10b-21 (Scrivener states that Gale omits vv.18-20)
John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτουργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day

ab.note: 1. συναξαριον ii: πεντηκοστη to υψωσις του σταυρου - ευαγγελια κατα ματθαιον - S2 W1 D2 - S2 W18 D1Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross. There are no John lections in this section of the Synaxarion

1. συναξαριον iii: υψωσις του σταυρου to αγια τεσσαρακοστη (νηστεια) - ευαγγελια κατα λουκαν - S3 W1 D2 to S3 W20 D1 Elevation of the Cross to Lent. There are no John lections in this section of the Synaxarion
1. συναξαριον iv: αγια και μεγαλη τεσσαρακοστη (νηστεια) - S4 W1 D2 to S4 W7 D1cLent to Holy Week
1. συναξαριον v: αγια και μεγαλη εβδομας - S5 W1 D2a - S5 W1 D7bHoly Week

div.note: John 19,38-42, John 21,14-25 (Scrivener and Ρinakes omit John 21,14-25)

ab.note: 3. Μηνολογιον: Ευαγγελια των ακινητων του ενιαυτου εορτων και τινων των καθ᾽ ημεραν αγιων

div.note: The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
Up to John 19, 35 in this manuscript.

ab.note: The next Johannine lection in this manuscript (the first reading of the 8th of May) has been written in a different hand; see supplement.

div.note: Manuscript ends at John 21, 7 – final pages missing
